paper_title: Study of Fossil Zokors and their Phylogeny

methods.description: Process of collecting fossil material from the ZD1001 locality through screen-washing and isolating specific specimens for study.

steps.description: Twenty-eight bags, about a half ton of matrix from the ZD1001 locality were screen-washed in 2010.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen-wash geological matrix from a specific locality to recover fossils
outputs: Washed sediment
Concentrated fossil material

steps.description: hundreds of remains of mammals were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fossil mammal remains from processed sediment
outputs: Collected mammal remains

steps.description: Of these 29 isolated molars belong to a fossil zokor.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and isolate specific fossil specimens (zokor molars) from collected remains
outputs: Isolated zokor molars (29 specimens)

steps.description: All specimens (listed below) are housed in the Institute of Vertebrate Paleontology and Paleoanthropology, Chinese Academy of Sciences in Beijing, and are available for examination by qualified researchers.
steps.searchable_summary: Store fossil specimens in a designated repository and make them available for study
inputs: Isolated zokor molars and other collected specimens
outputs: Specimens stored in IVPP collection

methods.description: Detailed examination, description, and precise measurement of fossil zokor molars using microscopy, following established terminology.

steps.description: Terms for dentition and measurement are adopted from Zheng et al. [20].
steps.searchable_summary: Define anatomical and measurement terms based on a reference standard
outputs: Standardized terminology for dentition and measurement

steps.description: Teeth were measured with a Wild Heerbrugg microscope to the nearest 0.01 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure dimensions of fossil teeth using a microscope with specified precision
outputs: Raw measurement data (to 0.01 mm precision)

steps.description: All specimens described in this paper are stored in the Institute of Vertebrate Paleontology and Paleoanthropology.
steps.searchable_summary: Confirm storage location of specimens used for description (already covered in collection method, but repeated here)
outputs: Confirmation of specimen storage location (IVPP)

steps.description: Description. M1. The anterior lobe (AL) is the narrowest portion... M2. The outline is typically orthomegodont... M3. It is similar to M2 in shape... m1. The labial salient angles are round... m2. The tooth has 3 salient and 2 reentrant angles... m3. The tooth is similar to m2 in shape...
steps.searchable_summary: Describe detailed morphological features of individual fossil molars (M1, M2, M3, m1, m2, m3)
inputs: Observations from examination (occlusal view, lateral view, angles, lobes, dentine tracts, roots, etc.)
outputs: Written descriptions of molar morphology

methods.description: Comparison of the Zanda zokor sample's morphological characteristics, size, and dentine tract parameters with previously described zokor species and existing classifications to determine taxonomic relationships.

steps.description: In the Zanda zokor, the M1 occlusal pattern is orthomegodont type; the anterior cap (ac) on m1 is relatively small, symmetrical and medially seated, and bra2 and lra3 on m1 are oppositely arrayed. ... zero-value dentine tract parameter a.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize the Zanda sample based on key morphological features and dentine tract parameters
inputs: Dentine tract parameters for Zanda sample
outputs: List of key characteristics for the Zanda sample (e.g., M1 occlusal pattern, m1 ac, bra2/lra3 arrangement, dentine tract parameter 'a', rooted molars)

steps.description: According to the diagnosis by Zheng [1,38], Zanda zokor should belong to the convex-skulled group. ... Molars of the Zanda zokor are rooted, which excludes the latter two genera. Among the three genera with rooted molars, the validity of Myotalpavus and Pliosiphneus are still questionable. ... These characters are consistent with the diagnosis for convex-skulled group by Zheng [1,38] and more specifically, for Prosiphneus as emended by Zheng et al. [20] and Liu et al. [33].
steps.searchable_summary: Compare sample characteristics against taxonomic diagnoses and classifications from reference literature
outputs: Assessment of how Zanda sample fits into existing classifications (e.g., convex-skulled group, genus Prosiphneus)

steps.description: In size, the Zanda sample is distinctly larger than teeth of P. qiui ... and P. haoi ... and slightly larger than P. licenti ... P. murinus ... and P. eriksoni ... and close to P. tianzuensis ... and P. cf. P. eriksoni ... (Fig 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare sample tooth measurements to published measurements of other species
inputs: Published measurement data for other species (from literature, potentially Fig 3)
outputs: Assessment of size similarity/difference between Zanda sample and other species

steps.description: In morphology, the Zanda sample differs from the primitive and lower-crowned species P. qiui and P. haoi by its remarkably higher crown or higher values of dentine tract parameters, highly fused roots, deeper labial reentrant angles, and rounder salient angles on m1. It also differs from P. licenti and P. murinus mainly by having high-value dentine tract parameters on the molars. Zanda sample can be distinguished from P. tianzuensis by having distinctly higher values of dentine tract parameters on upper teeth, and more symmetrical and medially seated ac on m1. Though of similar size, the Zanda sample differs from Prosiphneus cf. P. eriksoni from Bilike and Kunlun Pass by having lower values of dentine tract parameters on molars (Fig 4).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare sample morphological features and dentine tract parameters to published descriptions/data of other species
inputs: Morphological descriptions and dentine tract parameters for Zanda sample
Published descriptions/data for other species (from literature, potentially Fig 4)
outputs: Assessment of morphological similarity/difference between Zanda sample and other species

steps.description: In dental morphology, existing material from Zanda is essentially identical to Prosiphneus eriksoni from Ertemte, Nei Mongol. The minor differences between Ertemte and Zanda samples are the former having smaller sized molars with slightly lower b-index of m1 and higher Cindex of M1.
steps.searchable_summary: Synthesize comparison results to determine taxonomic placement and relationships, concluding on similarity to specific species
inputs: Results from size and morphological comparisons
outputs: Conclusion on the taxonomic identity and relationships of the Zanda sample (e.g., similarity to P. eriksoni)

methods.description: Phylogenetic analysis using cladistic methods to infer relationships among Prosiphneus species and related taxa based on a character matrix derived from dental features.

steps.description: Democricetodon lindsayi Qiu, 1996 is selected as an outgroup. ... a total of 12 taxa including all 7 known species of Prosiphneus plus Zanda zokor and Plesiodipus leei, Pl. progressus, Gobicricetodon flynni and G. robustus in Qiu [37] are chosen as ingroups.
steps.searchable_summary: Select taxa for phylogenetic analysis, including outgroup and ingroups
inputs: List of potential taxa (Prosiphneus species, Zanda zokor, Plesiodipus species, Gobicricetodon species, Democricetodon lindsayi)
outputs: List of 13 selected taxa for analysis (1 outgroup, 12 ingroups)

steps.description: we thus constructed a character matrix based on teeth only (Tables 3-5). Our 13 taxa by 55 characters (see S1 Appendix) data matrix was generated...
steps.searchable_summary: Create a character matrix for phylogenetic analysis based on dental features for selected taxa
inputs: List of 55 dental characters (from S1 Appendix)
Character states for each taxon (based on observation/literature)
outputs: Character matrix (13 taxa x 55 characters)

steps.description: Our 13 taxa by 55 characters (see S1 Appendix) data matrix was generated using the Mesquite ver. 3.02 (build 681) [43].
steps.searchable_summary: Format and prepare the character matrix using Mesquite software
outputs: Data matrix file in a format compatible with TNT (e.g., nexus)

steps.description: Cladistic analysis was performed using TNT (ver. 1.1, March, 2015) by Goloboff et al. [42].
steps.searchable_summary: Run phylogenetic analysis using TNT software
outputs: Results of the cladistic analysis (trees, statistics)

steps.description: Implicit Enumeration (IE, or branch-and-bound) search method under non-additive assumption was used to obtain an exact solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure phylogenetic search parameters in TNT (Implicit Enumeration, non-additive assumption)
outputs: Cladistic analysis results based on specified parameters

steps.description: The IE recovered 4 equally most parsimonious trees.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and retrieve the most parsimonious trees from the analysis results
outputs: Set of equally most parsimonious trees (4 trees)

steps.description: The strict consensus tree has a length of 122 steps, with Consistency Index = 0.869 and Retention Index = 0.888.
steps.searchable_summary: Compute the strict consensus tree from the set of most parsimonious trees and report its statistics
inputs: Set of equally most parsimonious trees
outputs: Strict consensus tree
Tree statistics (length, CI, RI)

steps.description: According to the strict consensus tree (Fig 5), all seven species of Prosiphneus and P. eriksoni of Zanda form a monophyletic clade. Plesiodipus leei+Pl. progressus form a clade of its own and is the sister group of Prosiphneus. Gobicricetodon robustus+G. flynni is a more basal clade outside the Plesiodipus+Prosiphneus group. Within Prosiphneus, relationship ... is unstable, and similarly within-species relationship ... is not fully resolved. These unresolved nodes notwithstanding, a basic relationship emerges that P. qiui is the most primitive species, and after several transitional species, P. eriksoni of Zanda is a terminal taxon of Prosiphneus.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret the phylogenetic relationships depicted in the strict consensus tree
outputs: Description and interpretation of phylogenetic relationships (clades, sister groups, basal groups, unresolved nodes, species relationships)
